mechanism_narrative: Rapid unemployment rate increases signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations decline. The mechanism operates continuously as unemployment is a real-time labor market signal.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 14.7% as of April 2020 reflects COVID-19 shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE increases by >0.5pp month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as the discount rate floor is reached and forward earnings growth becomes the dominant return driver. The mechanism is regime-specific to zero-lower-bound conditions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% or t-stat < 1.5) when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or mechanism fails to outperform baseline by >50bps Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand destruction that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone because it confirms demand weakness across multiple consumer-facing sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines >5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 403,946M and HOUST at 1,216K as of March 2020
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized consumer contraction
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces negative Rank IC (< -0.02) or fails to outperform single-series RSAFS or HOUST signals by >20bps in 3-year rolling window. SPX returns are not significantly lower when both triggers activate versus when only one activates.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines signal reduced manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. This constrains corporate profitability and signals recession risk. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal without requiring a specific threshold crossing.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.59 as of April 2020, down from pre-COVID levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (IC > -0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns following INDPRO declines are not lower than baseline by >30bps.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines signal labor market deterioration and reduced household income. This mechanism is triggered when monthly payroll losses exceed 500K persons, indicating shock-driven employment destruction. Under this threshold condition, equity valuations compress due to earnings and consumption headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,045K as of April 2020, down sharply from February 2020
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following >500K monthly payroll losses are not significantly negative (mean < -1% or t-stat < -1.5), or mechanism produces Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary accommodation via low federal funds rates jointly determine real discount rates. When CPI declines (deflation risk) while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates turn negative, supporting equity valuations through reduced real cost of capital. The interaction captures the joint effect of monetary and price dynamics.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 255.90 and FEDFUNDS at 0.05% as of April 2020
regime_hint: deflationary environment with zero-bound monetary policy
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 or fails to outperform single-series FEDFUNDS signal by >15bps. SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher when both deflation and zero-rates conditions hold versus when only one holds.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly signals aggregate demand strength and earnings growth capacity. Declining GDP growth expectations trigger equity repricing as forward earnings multiples compress. This mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal based on the most recent quarterly GDP release.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,987.88B (Q4 2019 annualized) as of decision date
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns following GDP growth acceleration are not higher than baseline by >50bps.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production and payroll employment jointly signal supply-side capacity and labor availability. When both decline simultaneously, it indicates broad-based supply destruction that constrains production capacity and earnings growth. The interaction effect captures synchronized supply-side and labor market weakness that is more predictive than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines >1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines >300K persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show sharp declines in March-April 2020 COVID shock
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 or fails to outperform single-series INDPRO or PAYEMS signals by >25bps in 3-year rolling window. SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower when both triggers activate versus when only one activates (t-test p > 0.10).
